what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block item that presents a ranked table of integration risks and a skeptical verdict about using Obsidian as a two-way host or co-writer of the live canonical markdown store.
observations: YAML frontmatter carries id, address, type=block, source=claude_code, channel=dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-48cd6801.
The body is a numbered risk table with Severity labels: Severe, High, Medium-low, and Non-issue.
Top severe risks are two-writer races, frontmatter schema drift, and YAML mangle causing get_item to raise and a node to vanish from the master.
It notes that typed address-edges are not wikilinks, so Obsidian graph/backlinks largely miss the real edges.
Mobile sync conflicts are flagged as high severity because last-writer-wins can rewind append-only state/history.
The verdict recommends a one-way, regenerated, read-only projection vault rather than Obsidian as a co-writer of canonical files.
standouts: The core verdict is negative: Obsidian should not be a two-way host, only a read-only regenerated projection.
The table explicitly says the marquee graph/backlinks benefit does not apply because typed address-edges are invisible to Obsidian.
It cites external evidence (cc_board.py and the live store) to support the risk claims.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with id, address, type, source, channel, links, and history frontmatter.
Uses a markdown table for structured risk presentation.
References another board item via a typed part_of link.
Labels risks with qualitative severity tiers.
concerns: The severity rankings are qualitative and the criteria are not defined in the file.
It references external evidence (cc_board.py, live store) without reproducing that evidence inline.
The language is conclusory and argumentative, which may obscure the underlying factual claims.
notes: A skeptical bottom-line risk assessment that argues Obsidian should be a read-only projection of the canonical store, never a co-writer.
questions: What is the exact Obsidian integration design being evaluated?
What specific evidence in cc_board.py supports each listed risk?
What is the append-only state/history mechanism that mobile sync could rewind?
What does 'typed address-edges' mean in this codebase?
What is item-48cd6801 and how does this item relate to it?
What does 'P15' denote in the title?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title and heading say 'Ranked bottom line', and the file delivers a ranked risk table plus a bottom-line verdict.
apparent_themes: Separation of canonical store from presentation layer
intention_signals: It is trying to push the project away from a two-way Obsidian-as-host integration and toward a one-way, regenerated, read-only Obsidian vault.
novelty: It is a deliberately skeptical, verdict-oriented noticeboard item that uses a ranked risk table to argue against an apparent integration direction.
contribution: It contributes a documented set of integration hazards and a conservative read-only-projection stance that a fused system would keep as a risk position and decision rationale.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-5a25262b is a ranked risk assessment arguing that using Obsidian as a two-way co-writer of the live canonical markdown store would cause severe write races, frontmatter drift, YAML parse failures, and graph/backlink mismatches, and recommending a one-way regenerated read-only projection instead.
intention_for_embedding: To record a skeptical bottom-line evaluation of Obsidian-as-host integration and steer the project toward a read-only projection model.
